stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Cyclotrons are circular particle accelerators that produce streams of high-energy particles.
  • Cyclotron beams are among the most precisely controlled particle streams ever produced.
  • Cyclotron beams are valuable tools for researchers studying nuclear reactions.
  • The first cyclotron built specifically to produce medical isotopes was completed in 1955.
  • It was called Crocker and was located near the University of California–Berkeley.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the location of the first cyclotron built to produce medical isotopes. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Cyclotron beams are among the most precisely controlled particle streams ever produced, making them valuable for researchers studying nuclear reactions.
The first cyclotron built specifically to produce medical isotopes, Crocker, was located near the University of California–Berkeley.
Cyclotrons are circular particle accelerators that produce streams of high-energy particles useful for studying nuclear reactions.
Crocker, the first cyclotron built specifically to produce medical isotopes, was completed in 1955.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. After identifying Crocker as the first cyclotron built to produce medical isotopes, the sentence’s main clause emphasizes its location.

Choice A describes cyclotron beams generally without mentioning Crocker’s location. Choice C describes cyclotrons generally without identifying or locating Crocker. Choice D identifies Crocker and gives its year of completion but not its location.
